name: Fudge
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup strained unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch process)
2 ounces (generous 1/2 cup) walnuts, cut or broken into medium-size pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously butter the sides of a 2 1/2- to 3-quart saucepan.
2. Mix the sugar, cocoa, salt, cream, and corn syrup in the pan.
3. Stir over moderate heat until the sugar is dissolved and the mixture comes to a boil.
4. Cover the saucepan for 2 or 3 minutes (steam from covering dissolves any clinging sugar granules).
5. Uncover and place a candy thermometer in the pan.
6. Boil without stirring until the thermometer reaches 236°F (the soft boil stage).
7. Do not stir, mix, shake, or disturb the mixture.
8. Remove the saucepan from heat; do not remove the thermometer.
9. Add butter to the pan and let it melt.
10. Let stand until temperature drops to 110°F.
11. Prepare a small loaf pan (e.g., 8 x 4-inch) by folding two pieces of aluminum foil inside.
12. When fudge cools to 110°F, remove thermometer.
13. Add vanilla; beat the fudge with a wooden spatula or spoon until it is thick and glossy.
14. Stir in nuts; push mixture into lined pan.
15. Cut fudge into portions (e.g., 12 large squares).
16. Wrap each portion individually in cellophane or wax paper, or store in an airtight container.
17. Fudge is best fresh but will keep for a few days at room temperature if well wrapped; it can be frozen for longer storage.
